name: cmi-docx
documentation:
# CMI-docx

[![Build](https://github.com/childmindresearch/cmi-docx/actions/workflows/test.yaml/badge.svg?branch=main)](https://github.com/childmindresearch/cmi-docx/actions/workflows/test.yaml?query=branch%3Amain)
[![codecov](https://codecov.io/gh/childmindresearch/cmi-docx/branch/main/graph/badge.svg?token=22HWWFWPW5)](https://codecov.io/gh/childmindresearch/cmi-docx)
[![Ruff](https://img.shields.io/endpoint?url=https://raw.githubusercontent.com/astral-sh/ruff/main/assets/badge/v2.json)](https://github.com/astral-sh/ruff)
![stability-stable](https://img.shields.io/badge/stability-stable-green.svg)
[![LGPL--2.1 License](https://img.shields.io/badge/license-LGPL--2.1-blue.svg)](https://github.com/childmindresearch/cmi-docx/blob/main/LICENSE)
[![pages](https://img.shields.io/badge/api-docs-blue)](https://childmindresearch.github.io/cmi-docx)

`cmi-docx` is a Python library for manipulating .docx files built on top of `python-docx`. It extends the functionality of `python-docx` by providing additional features and utilities for working with .docx files.

## Features

- Find and replace.
- Insert paragraphs in the middle of a document.
- Manipulate styles and formatting of paragraphs' substrings.

## Installation

Install this package from pypi using your favorite package manager. For example, using `pip`:

```sh
pip install cmi-docx
```

## Quick start

The following example demonstratesa few features of cmi-docx:

```Python
import docx

from cmi_docx import document

doc = docx.Document()
paragraph = doc.add_paragraph("Hello, world!")
extend_document = document.ExtendDocument(doc)
extend_paragraph = document.ExtendParagraph(paragraph)

# Find and replace text.
extend_document.replace("Hello", "Hi", {"bold": True})

# Insert and image
extend_document.insert_image(index=1, image_path="path/to/image.png")

# Reformat a paragraph
extend_paragraph.format(italics=True)
```

modules.name: cmi_docx.run
documentation: Module for extending python-docx Run objects.

classes.name: cmi_docx.run.FindRun

methods.name: cmi_docx.run.FindRun.__init__
documentation:
Initializes a FindRun object.

Args:
    paragraph: The paragraph containing the text.
    run_indices: The run indices of the text needle's start and end.
    character_indices: The character indices of the text in the runs.

methods.name: cmi_docx.run.FindRun.runs
documentation: Returns the runs containing the text.

methods.name: cmi_docx.run.FindRun.replace
documentation:
Replaces the text in the runs with the replacement text.

Args:
    replace: The text to replace.
    style: The style to apply to the replacement text. If None, the
        replacement text will have the same style as the first character of the
        original text.

methods.name: cmi_docx.run.FindRun._replace_without_style
documentation:
Replaces the text in the runs with the replacement text.

Args:
    replace: The text to replace.

methods.name: cmi_docx.run.FindRun._replace_with_style
documentation:
Replaces the text in the runs with the replacement text and style.

Args:
    replace: The text to replace.
    style: The style to apply to the replacement text.

methods.name: cmi_docx.run.FindRun.__lt__
documentation:
Sorts FindRun in order of appearance in the paragraph.

Makes FindRun objects sortable.

Args:
    other: The other FindRun object.

Returns:
    True if the character index of the first run is less than the
    character index of the other run.

Data class for maintaing find results in runs.

Attributes:
    paragraph: The paragraph containing the text.
    run_indices: The run indices of the text needle's start and end.
    character_indices: The character indices of the text in the runs.
        The first index is the start of the text in the first run containing
        the text. The second index is the end of the text in the last run.

methods.name: cmi_docx.run.ExtendRun.__init__
documentation:
Initializes an ExtendRun object.

Args:
    run: The run to extend.

methods.name: cmi_docx.run.ExtendRun.format
documentation:
Formats a run in a Word document.

Args:
    style: The style to apply to the run.

methods.name: cmi_docx.run.ExtendRun.get_format
documentation:
Returns the formatting of the run.

Returns:
    The formatting of the run.
Extends a python-docx Word run with additional functionality.

modules.name: cmi_docx.table
documentation: Extends a python-docx Table cell with additional functionality.

functions.name: cmi_docx.table.rgb_to_hex
documentation:
Converts RGB values to a hexadecimal color code.

Args:
    r: The red component of the RGB color.
    g: The green component of the RGB color.
    b: The blue component of the RGB color.

Returns:
    The hexadecimal color code representing the RGB color.

classes.name: cmi_docx.table.ExtendCell

methods.name: cmi_docx.table.ExtendCell.__init__
documentation:
Initializes an ExtendCell object.

Args:
    cell: The cell to extend.

methods.name: cmi_docx.table.ExtendCell.format
documentation:
Formats a cell in a Word table.

Args:
    style: The style to apply to the cell.
Extends a python-docx Word cell with additional functionality.

modules.name: cmi_docx.paragraph
documentation: Module for extending python-docx Paragraph objects.

classes.name: cmi_docx.paragraph.FindParagraph
documentation:
Data class for maintaing find results in paragraphs.

Attributes:
    paragraph: The paragraph containing the text.
    character_indices: A list of matching character indices of the text in the
        paragraph.

classes.name: cmi_docx.paragraph.ExtendParagraph

methods.name: cmi_docx.paragraph.ExtendParagraph.__init__
documentation:
Initializes an ExtendParagraph object.

Args:
    paragraph: The paragraph to extend.

methods.name: cmi_docx.paragraph.ExtendParagraph.find_in_paragraph
documentation:
Finds the indices of a text relative to the paragraph.

Args:
    needle: The text to find.

Returns:
    The indices of the text in the paragraph.

methods.name: cmi_docx.paragraph.ExtendParagraph.find_in_runs
documentation:
Finds the indices of a text relative to the paragraph's runs.

Args:
    needle: The text to find.

Returns:
    The indices of the text in the paragraph.

methods.name: cmi_docx.paragraph.ExtendParagraph.replace
documentation:
Finds and replaces text in a Word paragraph.

Args:
    needle: The text to find.
    replace: The text to replace.
    style: The style to apply to the replacement text.

methods.name: cmi_docx.paragraph.ExtendParagraph.insert_run
documentation:
Inserts a run into a paragraph.

Args:
    index: The index to insert the run at.
    text: The text of the run.
    style: The style of the run, see run.ExtendRun.format for more details.

Returns:
    The inserted run.

methods.name: cmi_docx.paragraph.ExtendParagraph.format
documentation:
Formats a paragraph in a Word document.

Args:
    style: The style to apply to the paragraph.
Extends a python-docx Word paragraph with additional functionality.

modules.name: cmi_docx.comment
documentation:
Module for handling Word comments.

Code based on sample code in https://github.com/python-openxml/python-docx/issues/93.

functions.name: cmi_docx.comment.add_comment

type.name:
tuple[paragraph.Paragraph | run.Run, paragraph.Paragraph | run.Run]
    | paragraph.Paragraph
    | run.Run

documentation:
Adds a comment to Word document.

There is a known bug where a range of locations can be provided
where the start comes after the end.

Args:
    docx_doc: A Word document.
    location: The paragraph and/or run object to place the comment on.
        May also be a tuple of these where the first element is the start
        and the second element is the end.
    author: Name of the comment author.
    text: Content of the comment.

modules.name: cmi_docx.styles
documentation: Style interfaces for document properties.

classes.name: cmi_docx.styles.RunStyle
documentation: Dataclass for run style arguments.

classes.name: cmi_docx.styles.ParagraphStyle

classes.name: cmi_docx.styles.TableStyle
documentation: Dataclass for table style arguments.

modules.name: cmi_docx.document
documentation: Extends a python-docx Word document with additional functionality.

classes.name: cmi_docx.document.ExtendDocument

methods.name: cmi_docx.document.ExtendDocument.__init__
documentation: Initializes a DocxSearch object for finding text.

methods.name: cmi_docx.document.ExtendDocument.find_in_paragraphs
documentation:
Finds the indices of a text relative to the paragraphs.

Args:
    needle: The text to find.

Returns:
    The indices of the text in the document.

methods.name: cmi_docx.document.ExtendDocument.find_in_runs
documentation:
Finds the indices of a text relative to the document's runs.

Args:
    needle: The text to find.

Returns:
    The locations of the text in the runs.

methods.name: cmi_docx.document.ExtendDocument.replace
documentation:
Finds and replaces text in a Word document.

Args:
    needle: The text to find.
    replace: The text to replace.
    style: The style to apply to the replacement text.

methods.name: cmi_docx.document.ExtendDocument.insert_paragraph_by_text
documentation:
Inserts a paragraph into a document.

Args:
    index: The index to insert the paragraph at.
    text: The text to insert.
    style: The style to apply to the text.

Returns:
    The new paragraph.

methods.name: cmi_docx.document.ExtendDocument.insert_paragraph_by_object
documentation:
Inserts a paragraph into a document.

Args:
    index: The index to insert the paragraph at.
    paragraph: The paragraph to insert.

Returns:
    The new paragraph.

methods.name: cmi_docx.document.ExtendDocument.insert_image
documentation:
Inserts an image at a given paragraph index.

Args:
    index: The paragraph index to insert the image at.
    image_path: The path to the image to insert.
    width: The width of the image.
    height: The height of the image.

methods.name: cmi_docx.document.ExtendDocument.all_paragraphs
documentation: Returns all paragraphs in the document, including headers and footers.

methods.name: cmi_docx.document.ExtendDocument._insert_empty_paragraph
documentation:
Inserts an empty paragraph at a given index.

Args:
    index: The index to insert the paragraph at.
    style: The style to apply to the paragraph.

Returns:
    The new paragraph.
Extends a python-docx Word document with additional functionality.
